gaunt_hook.name: Gaunt Hook
gaunt_hook.flavor: A wolf-catcher's hook, kept keen by a hunger that never let it rest.

bog_iron_head.name: Bog-Iron Head
bog_iron_head.flavor: Dredged from the Reach. It rings wrong — a bell with water in its throat.

scavver_fang_head.name: Scavver-Fang Head
scavver_fang_head.flavor: Filed from something that learned to grow teeth only at the very end.

drowned_sickle.name: Drowned Sickle
drowned_sickle.flavor: Still curved for a harvest. The Reach took the field, not the habit.

cutthroat_point.name: Cutthroat's Point
cutthroat_point.flavor: A farmer's knife that learned a second, worse trade.

reliquary_edge.name: Reliquary Edge
reliquary_edge.flavor: A saint's shinbone, honed sharp. It was holy once; now it only cuts.

magistrate_gavel.name: Magistrate's Gavel
magistrate_gavel.flavor: It sentenced a city of no one. It still falls like a verdict.

charnel_cleaver.name: Charnel Cleaver
charnel_cleaver.flavor: Heavy enough to part bone. The troll that swung it kept the bones.

ashen_greathead.name: Ashen Greathead
ashen_greathead.flavor: Climb-stone, too heavy by half. It does not strike so much as arrive.

choir_pike.name: Choirmaster's Pike
choir_pike.flavor: A processional pike, raised once for show. It has found its true use.

ridge_breaker.name: Ridge-Breaker
ridge_breaker.flavor: Climb-stone shaped by something the size of a door. It breaks what it meets.

summit_splinter.name: Summit Splinter
summit_splinter.flavor: A shard off the Pall's own dark. It is very cold, and very sharp.

reed_shaft.name: Sodden Reed Shaft
reed_shaft.flavor: Light as a held breath. Cut to call holds to a supper that never came.

withe_haft.name: Witherwood Haft
withe_haft.flavor: Cut from a tree that has forgotten how to be wood — light, and patient.

gullet_bone.name: Gullet-Bone Haft
gullet_bone.flavor: A long bone from the throat of the rock. It does not tire.

iron_brace_haft.name: Iron-Braced Haft
iron_brace_haft.flavor: Banded for a grip that would not be struck loose. It never was.

weir_pole.name: Weir-Keeper's Pole
weir_pole.flavor: It tended the gates that starved the holds. It knows long, thankless work.

bellrope_grip.name: Bell-Rope Grip
bellrope_grip.flavor: Wound from the curfew bell's rope. It has never stopped wanting to be pulled.

census_rod.name: Census Rod
census_rod.flavor: The rod a clerk struck names with. It remembers every one it unmade.

procession_haft.name: Procession Haft
procession_haft.flavor: A bier-pole from a funeral that set out for the grave and never arrived.

censer_chain.name: Censer-Chain Haft
censer_chain.flavor: Wound from the chain an acolyte never set down. It still wants to swing.

warden_haft.name: Warden's Haft
warden_haft.flavor: The grip the last Warden's hand wore smooth. It knows how to hold on.

pilgrim_stave.name: Pilgrim's Stave
pilgrim_stave.flavor: It walked the whole road to the Pall, and back, and to the Pall again.

ashen_grip.name: Ashen Climb Grip
ashen_grip.flavor: Wrapped in hide off the slope. Whatever wore that hide is climbing still.

grave_iron_core.name: Grave-Iron Core
grave_iron_core.flavor: Pried from a tomb door. It guards from habit, having nothing left to guard.

pall_glass_core.name: Pall-Glass Core
pall_glass_core.flavor: A bead of the Pall, cooled hard. It still remembers being a wall.

warden_seal_core.name: Warden's Seal
warden_seal_core.flavor: The sigil the last Warden wore. It keeps whatever it is given.

census_core.name: Census-Stone Core
census_core.flavor: Chipped from the plinth where one unscratched name still stands.

bleeding_core.name: Bleeding-Edge Core
bleeding_core.flavor: It keeps an edge no whetstone ever gave it. Wounds it opens do not close.

bog_rot_core.name: Bog-Rot Core
bog_rot_core.flavor: Wet rot packed into the weapon's heart. What it touches begins to turn.

ember_core.name: Ember Core
ember_core.flavor: A coal off a pyre that has not gone cold in three long winters.

silt_core.name: Silt-Choke Core
silt_core.flavor: Packed with drowning-silt. What it strikes forgets how to strike back.

hexward_core.name: Hexward Core
hexward_core.flavor: A magistrate's seal. Marked by it, a thing is guilty, and easier to harm.

tolling_core.name: Tolling Core
tolling_core.flavor: It rings, once, when it bites. The sound stops whatever hears it.

grave_chill_core.name: Grave-Chill Core
grave_chill_core.flavor: Cold the way a tomb is cold. It leans on the arm that meets it.

pall_heart_core.name: Pall-Heart Core
pall_heart_core.flavor: A clot of the Pall itself. It is hungry, and it shares the hunger.

grief_sigil.name: Grief-Sigil
grief_sigil.flavor: A small grief. It does not weigh much, but it is yours to carry.

hold_fast_rune.name: Hold-Fast Rune
hold_fast_rune.flavor: The weir-keepers' mark for a sealed gate. It does not let things through.

sealed_gate_mark.name: Sealed-Gate Mark
sealed_gate_mark.flavor: The word for shut, cut deep. Nothing the steel guards gets past it.

mourners_mark.name: Mourner's Mark
mourners_mark.flavor: An old grief, cut deep into the steel. Grief, at least, endures.

census_line.name: An Unscratched Line
census_line.flavor: One line the clerk's nib spared. Someone, somewhere, is still counted.

choir_descant.name: The Choir's Descant
choir_descant.flavor: A high line of the rite, sung over the rest. It carries; it endures.

starveling_psalm.name: Starveling's Psalm
starveling_psalm.flavor: A hold's last prayer, learned by heart because there was nothing else.

unremembered_name.name: An Unremembered Name
unremembered_name.flavor: A name scored over and over — yet the steel beneath still holds its shape.

pall_ward_verse.name: Pall-Ward Verse
pall_ward_verse.flavor: A line said against the grey. It buys a breath, and another, and another.

warden_oath.name: Warden's Oath
warden_oath.flavor: The promise the Pall keeps its Wardens to. It is a heavy thing to wear.

last_breath_verse.name: Last-Breath Verse
last_breath_verse.flavor: The one line of the rite the fire spared. It keeps you here a while longer.

ash_remembrance.name: Ash-Remembrance
ash_remembrance.flavor: Cut deep, against forgetting. The deeper the cut, the longer it holds.
